observations.recommendation:
1.34 We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Human Resource
Department to exert earnest efforts in the processing of the immediate hiring and
appointment of the Certified Public Accountant to conduct a thorough
examination/analysis/review of the financial statements and verification of
the books of accounts of the Municipality to address the material discrepancies
and significant variances reported in the financial statements and henceforth,
prepare correct financial reports.

3.21 We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
1. Appoint a qualified employee to be the General Services Officer who:
(a) will take custody of and be accountable for all properties, real or
personal, owned by the local government unit and those granted to it in
the form of donation, reparation, assistance and counterpart of joint
projects; and (b) shall prepare and maintain the records on the
acquisition, description, custody, disposal and other information about
the property, plant and equipment based on the source documents of the
transactions in the Property Cards;
2. Instruct the Inventory Committee to:
a. Formulate a Physical Inventory Plan and schedule for the conduct of
physical inventory that will complement with the time of the members
of the committee in order not to prejudice the operations of the
Municipality. Also, conduct the physical count pursuant to 6.1 and 6.2
of COA Circular No. 2020-006; and
b. Cause the submission of a complete Report of the Physical Count of
Property, Plant and Equipment to the Audit Team not later than
January 31 of each year.
3. Require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Trace the differences in the account balances and reconcile her records
with the financial statements;
b. Act on the preparation and maintenance of the PPE ledger cards based
on the source documents of the transactions; and
c. Provide the necessary disclosures required in IPSAS 17 on PPE.

4.20 We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Record only those obligated transactions with complete supporting
documents and ensure that obligations are recognized in the books based
on the valid supporting documents;
b. Exert utmost effort on the retrieval of the documents supporting the
prior year balances of accounts payable and abnormal debit balances
amounting to P5,315,681.10 and P1,556,280.36, respectively, otherwise,
prepare adjustment entry for undocumented claims and payables;
c. Strictly comply with the COA Circular 2015-001 dated December 1,
2015 which prescribes the revised chart of accounts for Local Government
Units; and
d. Reconcile the material variances among the balances of the inter-agency
payables, intra-agency payables, trust liabilities, other payables and
guaranty deposit payables that exist among the trial balance, financial
statements and Notes to Financial Statements.

5.7 We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Accountant to reconcile the differences among the details/schedule, General
Ledger, Subsidiary Ledger, Statement of Financial Position, and Notes to
Financial Statement of the Due to NGA account as required by Section 121 of
Presidential Decree 1445 and Paragraph 27 of the International Public
Sector Accounting Standards.

5.8 During the exit conference, the newly hired Municipal Accountant agreed with
the recommendation of the Audit Team.

8.11 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer and Collecting Officers that the original copy of the official
receipts be written in hard indelible ballpen and the duplicate and triplicate
copies will be clear carbon reproductions in all respects of whatever may
have been written on the original and avoid alterations and/or erasures on
the official receipts.

8.12 During the exit conference, the Municipal Treasurer noted and agreed with the
recommendations of the Audit Team.

13.17 We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the:
1. Municipal Accountant to:
a. Record donations in cash under the Trust Liabilities – DRRMF
account and ensure that the donations are duly supported by Report
of Collection and Deposit (RCD) and supporting documents
submitted by the Municipal Treasurer, or based on the credit memo if
the donations are directly deposited by the donor to the agency bank
account;
b. Furnish the Budget Unit copies of the RCD, Journal Entry Voucher
(JEV) and credit memo as bases in the preparation of the Registry of
Cash Donations and Utilization (RCDU);
c. Coordinate with Municipal Budget Officer on the preparation of the
Report on the Receipts and Utilization of DRRMF sourced from GAA
and Cash Donations;
d. Require the CDUR, duly signed by the head of the requesting unit and
the budget officer, as attachment to the disbursement voucher on the
utilization of cash donations; and
e. Disclose all relevant information not evident on the face of the FS such
as but not limited to the donations received, utilization, and balances
in the Notes to Financial Statements.
2. Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Ensure the received donations in cash should be deposited in the
bank account exclusively maintained for the DRRM fund; and
b. Submit to the Accounting Office a separate RCD for DRRM and
other supporting documents such as Official Receipts and deposit
slips pertaining to the donations in cash received, for recording in the
books of accounts of the Municipality.
3. Municipal Budget Officer to:
a. Prepare RCDU per donor and per purpose, and timely update the
registry by posting the transactions based on the source documents
such as the RCD for the receipt and the CDUR for its utilization; and
b. Coordinate with the Accounting Unit on the preparation of the
Report on the Receipt and Utilization of DRRMF sourced from GAA
and Cash Donations.

13.18 During the exit conference, the newly hired Municipal Accountant explained that
coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Treasurer was
already made to implement the above-mentioned recommendations.

16.11 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Disaster
Risk Reduction and Management Council ensure that the municipality is
prepared for calamities as prescribed in NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 by procuring standard disaster response
equipment required in DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-73.

16.12 During the exit conference, the Local Chief Executive and the Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer, agreed with the implementation of the
recommendation of the Audit Team.

Cause the registration and transfer the land in the name of the Municipality. Compel the Municipal and Accountant to review the details of the Due from NGAs, Due from NGOs/POs and Other Receivable accounts and exert all efforts to locate records to prove the validity of claim from the identified recipients of these funds; and 2. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a. We recommended the Municipal Mayor to: 1. Conduct trainings and re-orientations on all Accounting Personnel regarding the basic processes, established standards, rules and regulations of the accounting system in the public sector; 3. Require the Accounting Section to immediately verify the differences in the balances between the General Ledger, Trial Balance and Financial Statements; and 4. Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain General Ledgers for all accounts, as well as Subsidiary Ledgers, if applicable. Ensure also that the account titles/names used and the amount which appears in the GL are the same with that of the SL and the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: 1. Conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the fund transfers; and 2. Reconcile the unliquidated fund transfers between the source and implementing government entities, prepare adjusting journal entries for the reconciling items noted, and require liquidation of balances; and 3. Prepare necessary adjusting entries for reconciling items; 4. Prepare aging of dormant receivables and unliquidated cash advances and fund transfers quarterly to support write-off requests.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: 1. Direct the General Services Officer (GSO) to train additional personnel to reinforce the inventory team to pave the way for the completion of the physical count of all properties and come up with the RPCPPE; 2. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the PPE Ledger Card (PPELC) for each class of PPE, listing the data on acquisition, description, custody, estimated life, rate of depreciation, disposal, and other information and drop from the books unserviceable properties after Waste Material Report and Inventory and Inspection Report are duly approved and evaluated by COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to exert effort in enforcing the immediate settlement of audit disallowances and to ensure that all employees who are retiring or transferring to other agencies shall first settle the disallowances and charges for which they are liable.
observations.reason: Not all disallowances and charges were settled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the SWM Focal Person to consider the social media as a favorable means of communication on information it wishes to pass on to the public. Create a link on the Municipality’s website for information and policy on solid waste management.
observations.reason: Proper communication channel is not established.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Solid Waste Management Focal Person to maintain disposal site records in the Residual Containment Area (RCA).
observations.reason: Disposal site records were not maintained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the establishment of a mechanism to measure the volume of wastes and type of waste disposed at the RCA to provide baseline data to assist in developing effective waste minimization strategies and future plans for waste management.
observations.reason: No mechanism to measure volume at RCA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that a proper gate at the entrance of the RCA must be built to control vehicle mobility, inspect the waste to be disposed then direct the vehicle to the designated area depending on the type of waste already inspected. Color coding is also recommended to ensure proper waste segregation.
observations.reason: Proper segregation of waste is not practiced.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the RCA be fenced with a secured gate at the entrance, to minimize illegal visitors or scavengers. This will also help address the occupational health and safety issues by limiting access.
observations.reason: RCA lacks fencing and secured gate.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Human Resource Management Officer to adhere to the provisions set forth in Section 77 of Republic Act No. 7160.
observations.reason: Job orders are hired because there are no qualified applicants for the position.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to expedite the processing of documents for the titling of lands owned by the Municipality and further recommended that before any construction is made, land ownership through a certificate of title is obtained.
observations.reason: Land titling is still on process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Executive direct the GAD Focal Person to establish a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation System.
observations.reason: GAD Monitoring and Evaluation System was not established.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to make reconciliation and verify who among the employees is deficient in paying the loan obligations.
observations.reason: Amounts could not be reconciled due to lack of records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a. Cause the creation of a task force that will take charge in the preparation of the BRS for the nine LBP accounts setting a time frame for its completion.
observations.reason: Not all bank reconciliation statements were prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 1) Direct the Property Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of PPE; 2) Direct the Inventory Committee to reconcile accounting records and actual inventory; and 3) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the PPE Ledger Card (PPELC) for each class of PPE.
observations.reason: Inventory committee lacked training and did not conduct physical count.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel the Municipal Accountant to review the details of the Due from NGAs, Due from LGUs and Other Receivable accounts and exert all efforts to settle dormant accounts and comply with reportorial requirements.
observations.reason: Dormant accounts remained unliquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Budget Officer to strictly adhere to Section 287 of RA 7160 and DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01 to ensure that only projects that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures and aligned with the purpose/objectives of the 20% Development Fund be funded from such appropriation.
observations.reason: Funds were not fully utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council to prepare and implement a comprehensive and integrated Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Plan that encompasses the four thematic areas of disaster risk reduction and management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Solid Waste Management Board to strictly implement feasible re-use, recycling, and composting programs and to include in its priority projects the procurement of a garbage truck and information dissemination campaign to adopt measures toward effective solid waste management at the barangay level. Also recommended that the Mayor provide necessary financial and technical support.
observations.reason: Solid Waste Management is not practiced and MRFs not established.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive consider creating and organizing an Internal Audit Services, in compliance with R.A. 4177 and AO 70 to provide an adequate internal control system.
observations.reason: Organization is not fully functioning due to lack of training of personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD Council to prioritize the implementation of the CBMS for the creation of a GAD database to serve as inputs or basis for planning, programming and policy formulation.
observations.reason: GAD database was not established.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned officials to prepare MDRRMFIP and include in the MDRRMFIP the unutilized portion of the 70% MOOE and 30% QRF of prior years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor provide the necessary financial and technical assistance to the barangays for the establishment and operation of functional and standard-based MRFs.

observations.recommendation:
1. Require the Municipal Accountant to review the current accounting and
reporting system of the Municipality to check and test whether it is in
accordance with established accounting standards, rules and regulations.
Immediately rectify all errors;
2. Conduct trainings and re-orientations on all Accounting Personnel
regarding the basic processes, established standards, rules and regulations
of the accounting system in the public sector;
3. Require the Accounting Section to immediately verify the differences in
the balances between the General Ledger, Trial Balance and Financial
Statements; and
4. Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain General Ledgers (GL) for
all accounts, as well as, Subsidiary Ledgers (SL), if applicable. Ensure also
that the account titles/names used and the amount which appears in the GL
are the same with that of the SL and the Financial Statements.

1. Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical count of PPEs and
prepare RPCPPE with complete data for each item. Also, furnish the Audit
Team a copy of the duly accomplished RPCPPE not later than January 31
of each year in accordance with the MNGAS for LGUs;
2. Municipal Accountant and Property custodian to maintain ledger cards and
property cards, respectively, and reconcile periodically; and
3. Municipal Accountant to trace the differences in the account balances and
reconcile all her records with the financial statements.

a. Prepare and certify a monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of Local
DRRM Fund;
b. Transfer immediately the cash corresponding to the unexpended balance
as of year-end of Local DRRM Fund for QRF and MOOE from General
Fund to Trust Fund account; and
c. Provide adequate disclosure in the Notes to FS for amount and details of
the unexpended balance of Local DRRM Fund.

10.7. We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant:


- to send within 10 days before the expiration of the prescribed period a written
reminder under signature of the Mayor or his duly authorized representative
enjoining officials and employees concerned to settle their cash advance;
- not to sign claims for additional cash advance until the previous cash
advance is settled;
- to cause the immediate withholding of salaries and claims of officers and
employees who failed to settle their cash advances within the prescribed
period after being sent notice and demand; and
- to furnish all department heads listings of all unliquidated cash advances of
employees under their charge to enhance efforts to effect liquidation.

11.7. We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant
to request for the write off of the aforementioned accounts in accordance with
COA Circular No, 2016-005 supported with an aging of dormant receivables
and fund transfers, indicating in the remarks column the existence of the
applicable conditions, as follows:
a. Absence of records or documents to validate/support the claim and/or
unreconciled reciprocal accounts
b. Death of the accountable officer/employee/debtor
c. Unknown whereabouts of the accountable officer/employee/debtor,
and that he/she could not be located despite diligent efforts to find
him/her
d. Exhaustion of all possible remedies by the Management to collect the
receivables and to demand liquidation of cash advances and fund
transfers
e. No pending case in court involving the subject dormant accounts.

12.12. We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the
existence of the fund transfers; and,
b. Reconcile the unliquidated fund transfers between the source and
accounting records, and, prepare the adjusting entries for the reconciling
items noted, and require liquidation of the balance.

to: a) confirm with the BIR of the veracity of the balances and make the

statements of the over-remittance, b) adapt monitoring mechanism to detect
variances in the amount to be remitted and withheld, and c) accounting
records such as cash receipts journal, check disbursement journal, cash
disbursement journal, general and subsidiary ledgers must be updated
monthly in order to determine the exact amount to be remitted to the Bureau
of Internal Revenue.

14.9. We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant
to a) confirm with the GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth the veracity of the

the amount to be remitted and withheld, and c) accounting records such as
cash receipts journal, check disbursement journal, cash disbursement journal,

immediately prepare Bank Reconciliation Statements on all bank accounts of the

3.3 We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
effect immediately the Adjusting Journal Entries (AJEs) for the provision of the
depreciation of the listed PPEs in consonance with the rules and regulations stated
in the IPSAS 17.

4.6.1 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned officials to
implement the projects listed under the appropriation ordinance so as to effect
timely delivery of services to the constituents.
4.6.2 Management was amendable to the recommendation.

5.5 We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the officials concerned to
implement the projects listed under the appropriation ordinance so as to effect
timely delivery of services to the constituents.

6.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
adopt stern regulations in the pursuit of zeroing-in the due and demandable cash
advances.

7.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer and
Municipal Social Work and Development Officer to fast-track the implementation
of programs, projects and activities funded by the BUB to provide constituents of
the maximum benefits due them.

8.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
to exert effort in enforcing on the immediate settlement of audit disallowances
and to ensure that all employees who are retiring or transferring to other agencies
shall first settle the disallowances and charges for which they are liable in
accordance with Section 7 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15,
2009.

9.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the SWM Focal Person to
consider the appropriate means of communication depending on the information
that it wishes to pass on to the public and its stakeholders. For instance, there
should be public notices on television and on the radio about who can access the
Residual Containment Area (RCA), the working hours at the area to limit after-
hours access and the schedule for collection of wastes from households for the
different zones. Websites have become increasingly effective and economical in
disseminating information. Therefore, it is important to have a specific link for
SWM within the Municipality’s website so that it is easier for the public to have
access to information and policy on solid waste management as well as any issue
concerning solid waste management.

9.7 To ensure effective implementation of the solid waste management plan, a well-
designed, current and targeted community awareness program is required. The
targeted audience should be considered when determining how the information
should be given to the public. The urgency of the matter should also be
considered so that everyone is aware of the matter as soon as possible.

10.4 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the SWM Focal Person to
maintain disposal site records at the RCA.

11.4 We recommended that a mechanism to measure the volume of waste and type of
waste should be placed at the site, which will provide baseline data to assist in
developing effective waste minimization strategies and future plans for waste
management.

12.6 We recommended that a proper gate at the entrance to the RCA must be built
so that it is easier for the personnel at the RCA to stop vehicles coming at the
gate, inspect the waste to confirm the type of waste to be disposed then direct the
vehicle to the designated area depending on the type of waste already inspected.
This daily process will compensate for the non-segregation of waste at source
and will ensure that wastes being disposed at the RCA are properly separated.
12.7 We further recommended that the SWM Focal Person should implement a
color coding or appropriate means of identification to ensure that wastes are
properly segregated at the RCA.

13.4 We recommended that the RCA be fenced with a secured gate at the entrance to
minimize illegal visitors or scavengers. This will also help address the occupational
health and safety issues by limiting access.

14.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Human Resource
Management officer to adhere to the provisions set forth in Section 77 of RA
No. 7160.

15.7 We recommended that Municipal Mayor exercise prudence in granting other
allowances despite an approved SB ordinance relative to such claim, because such
disbursement is subject to refund in the form of a Notice of Disallowance.

16.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to
expedite the processing of documents for the titling of lands owned by the
Municipality. We further recommend that before any construction is made,
land ownership thru a certificate of title is obtained.

17.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to
establish a VAW Help Desk in their respective barangays managed by a person
with competent personnel.

18.4 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to
establish a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation System.

19.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to reconcile her records with that of the GSIS. Furthermore, the municipal
accountant shall also verify who among the employees is deficient in paying its
loan obligations so that an adjustment in his/her deduction shall be made.

record the expense only upon issuance of RIS to the end user.

alleviation projects (PAPs) funded by BUB to provide constituents the benefits
due them.

officials to prepare MDRRMFIP and include in the MDRRMFIP the unutilized
portion of the 70% MOOE and 30% QRF of prior years.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the
corresponding disbursement vouchers together with the related supporting
documents pertaining to the ten (10) checks totaling P1,996,920.87.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the BAC to
review thoroughly the bidding documents submitted by the contractor as to its
legality, validity and completeness and to adhere strictly the provisions of the
Revised IRR of Republic Act (RA) 9184 and COA Circular No. COA Circular
No. 2009-001.

We recommended that LCE shall direct all official concerned to strictly
adhere to the provisions cited in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No.
2011-1 and exert all effort to implement all the projects budgeted under the
20% development plan.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Local
Treasurer and Accountant to observe strictly COA Circular No. 2012-001 and
that all fund transfers must have a Memorandum of Agreement to include a
provision that the infrastructure project must be validated upon completion
through conduct of inspection by the Municipal Engineer.

We recommended that the LCE direct all concerned employees to
adhere strictly to Section 53 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 which provides
the important steps in negotiated procurements and make sure that all that
invitation/request for proposal to those PhilGEPS registered suppliers and the
NOA are posted in the PhilGEPS.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned
personnel to follow the guidelines set forth in PD 1445 and COA Circular No.
94-013 .

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Assessor to exert
effort in the titling of lots in the name of the Municipality as proof of
ownership over the properties.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, being the chair of the Local
School Board strictly observe the provisions of Section 272 of RA 7160 and
DepEd Order No. 12, s. 2012.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive directs the Municipal
Budget Officer to prepare and maintain all the necessary registries and to
adhere to the provisions enlisted in Chapter 3 of the Government Accounting
Manual.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the provision of
financial and technical assistance to the barangays for the establishment and
operations of functional and standard-based MRFs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: cause the
discontinuance of the use of open dump sites and enjoin the Sanguniang Bayan
to pass an ordinance for “No open Burning” and “No Littering” policies.

We recommended the following remedial steps: Create a task force that will take charge in the preparation of the BRS for all funds and accounts; Make written representations with the depository banks to ensure prompt submission of the Bank statements; Maintain an updated SL which will show the details or breakdown of the balance of the controlling account appearing in the general ledger.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant prepared the Bank Reconciliati on Statement as of December 31, 2017 for all funds and accounts submitted to COA for review in the 1st quarter of CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: 1. The Municipal Mayor should adhere strictly to Section 89 of P.D. 1445; COA Circular 97-002; Sections 5.7 and 8, and COA Circular 2012-004 dtd. November 28, 2012 in the implementation of the rules and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances. 2. Direct the Accountant to monitor the liquidation of cash advances granted to officers/employees and prohibit the grant of additional cash advance pending liquidation of the previous cash advance. 3. After serving notice and demand settlement of the outstanding cash advances, the accountant is now mandated to submit the supporting documents to COA for submission to higher authorities. To enhance efforts to effect liquidation, furnish division chiefs a listing of long-outstanding unliquidated cash advances of employees under their charge, as well as posting to the bulletin board the relevant data on this issue.

Since responsibility rests upon the Head of the Agency, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Accountant to submit the accounts of the municipality regularly on every 10th day of the succeeding month. Likewise all accountable officers are to submit reports and source documents within five (5) days after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: Since fiscal 2014 the Accountant was directed to submit the accounts of the municipality regularly on every 10th day of the succeeding month.
observations.reason: Report, journals and all paid DVs were still not regularly submitted. As of December 31, 2016, the Accountant had submitted journal and paid DVs for the period from January 01 to May 31, 2016 causing a delay of up to six months.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management fully adhere with the limitation imposed under Section 325a of the Local Government Code to avoid disallowances in budget review and audit. Results of the review by the Provincial Budget Office and the Sangguniang Panlalawigan should be fully considered with recommendations be implemented.
observations.reason: The LGU employees had started their partial payment on the 4th Tranche Salary Adjustment and the CY 2016 review by the Provincial Budget Office and the Sangguniang Panlalawigan was done. There still existed the 55% PS limitation exceeded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended strict adherence to COA Circular 2012-001 and Annex E of the IRR of RA 9184 requiring checking of documents as to completeness before claims are paid. Carry out measures to preclude suspension or disallowance during post-audit and ensure required documents are attached.
observations.reason: There were payments made; some documents required in the observations were later submitted. Some required documents were still lacking.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU comply with the requirements for registration and insurance of the PCSO ambulance unit and exert efforts to obtain records/documents necessary to facilitate the registration and insurance of the unit.
observations.reason: Deed of donation was not an original document; efforts made to obtain documents necessary to register the unit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee/Chairman/Secretariat to furnish COA with copies of the perfected contracts/purchase orders together with complete supporting documents within five (5) working days from issuance for COA review.
observations.reason: Contracts/purchase documents were submitted to COA for review.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure the funding agencies immediately liquidate transferred funds to concerned government agencies. Order the return of the unused monies where applicable and send letters to defaulting agencies requesting submission of necessary documents proving utilization or non-use of transferred funds. If papers can no longer be recovered, request for write-off of accounts be sent to the Commission Proper so that adjustments in recording on the agency’s books of accounts may be effected.
observations.reason: Some liquidations were submitted; responsibility for liquidation rests with the funding agencies. Return of unspent monies was pursued where possible.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to observe the guidelines in the utilization of the 20% EDF to attain the maximum benefit envisioned under the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-01 and install appropriate controls over the proper utilization of the 20% EDF.
observations.reason: There were still expenses charged to 20% EDF which are not authorized. Some corrective measures implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Mayor and the Vice-Mayor exercise prudence in the usage of government funds, make representation with the PCL officials and request that participants come from the province in order to minimize cost; send participants only to seminars with agenda that is their area of concern; proportionate amount pertaining to actual days when government transportation facilities were used should be deducted from their monthly Transportation Allowances; each trip ticket be properly accomplished and the authorized passenger officials be indicated.
observations.reason: Current LCE had marked vehicles 'FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY' and adhered to Section 56 guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting Office, Bids and Awards Committee and the Technical Working Group undertake scrutiny before bank instruments are accepted; cause the blacklisting of the contractor if further scrutiny shows that the performance bond is spurious; and pursue administrative and criminal charges where evidence warrants.
observations.reason: The LGU caused scrutiny and will pursue appropriate actions including blacklisting if warranted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, being the chair of the Local School Board, ensure that SEF expenditures comply with Section 272 of RA 7160 and DepEd guidelines and that charging of non-priority items cease.
observations.reason: The Local School Board made sure that subsequent charges to SEF were compliant with rules; some prior unauthorized charges remained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that tax collection efforts be intensified using remedies under the Local Government Code such as auction, levy and distraint of personal property; increase LGU’s collection through tax information campaign and rewards; publication of delinquent properties and sending billing notices to defaulting taxpayers.
observations.reason: Remedial measures were initiated in CY 2016 including auction and levy steps; uncollectible taxes of prior years still existed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that all projects be meticulously planned and judiciously implemented by the municipality; Program of Works and Bill of Materials shall be prepared by the Municipal Engineer with detailed engineering before procurement process starts; establish a Local Inspectorate Team to conduct honest inspection on all requests for payment by contractors.
observations.reason: The municipality established procedures and oversight to ensure proper project documentation and inspection.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer not certify Disbursement Vouchers if the performance bond from the contractor is not attached; Performance Bond shall be required before all contracts are executed.
observations.reason: Performance bonds were thereafter required for contracts to secure the interest of the government.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Utilization Report on the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor for evaluation as to the proper utilization and disbursement of disaster funds.
observations.reason: The Local report was prepared and submitted to COA but some elements of utilization and disbursement documentation were not on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor and Vice-Mayor put proper marking on all motor vehicles owned by the LGU and bear government plates pursuant to COA Circular No. 75-6 dated November 7, 1975.
observations.reason: Vehicles were marked 'FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY' and government plates were affixed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that contracts for job order workers contain clear statements of duties and responsibilities or equivalent terms to serve as standard with which to base the evaluation of their duties and responsibilities.
observations.reason: HRMO had included statements of service in the revised Job Order contracts to serve as standards.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee and Secretariat ensure that authenticated copies of all bidding documents are submitted and that illegible submissions be refused acceptance; authenticated photocopies should be clear and legible.
observations.reason: BAC certified the authenticity of all submitted documents and COA received authenticated copies for review.
